goal: Land three agreed no-client-input wins before Wency's July office visit: remove the prep page, add a /tasks scheduled-tasks oversight page over the existing reminders + scheduled-email backend, and ship a real PWA manifest so the app installs standalone on iPhone.
why: These are the three agreed, low-risk wins the client can see immediately at the July office visit; they need zero client input and de-risk the larger invoice-automation phases that follow.
success_criteria: /prep returns 404; no /prep link in Nav, mobile drawer, or Footer; .prep-grid styles, the draft API route, and the now-orphaned src/lib/agents/draftEmail.ts are deleted; tsc confirms no broken import and the agent tool registry is unchanged.

/tasks renders a merged reminders+scheduled-emails timeline (status, type, mailbox/owner, recurrence) with a working principal-scoped cancel hitting DELETE /api/tasks/cancel; both new routes are auth-gated and principal-scoped; the page has loading, error, and empty states.
public/manifest.json is served (name, start_url:/, display:standalone, 192/512 icons); layout.tsx emits the manifest link + apple-mobile-web-app meta; iOS add-to-home-screen launches standalone.

tasks.title: Remove the prep page (page, links, CSS, draft route, orphaned lib)
acceptance_criteria: No 'Prep' link in the desktop nav rail, mobile drawer, or footer; all show a 'Tasks' link to /tasks.
.prep-grid and the EMAIL PREP comment block no longer exist in globals.css.
The draft API route and src/lib/agents/draftEmail.ts are deleted; tsc reports no broken import.
No agent tool definition references draftEmail (17-tool registry unchanged).
tasks.action: Delete src/app/prep/page.tsx, src/app/api/outlook/draft/route.ts, and src/lib/agents/draftEmail.ts (draftEmail is referenced only by the draft route and prep page, zero hits in the agent tool registry). Edit the LINKS array in Nav.tsx (remove the /prep entry, add a /tasks 'Tasks' entry after Finance) and in Footer.tsx (remove /prep, add /tasks). Remove the EMAIL PREP block in globals.css: the comment, the .prep-grid rule, and its 720px media query, leaving the Panels block intact.

tasks.title: Add the two principal-scoped tasks API routes (list + cancel) over the existing backend
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/tasks/list with a valid session returns { ok:true, data:TaskItem[] } each carrying kind (reminder|email), id, status, scheduledAt, recurrence, and a who/what label, sorted by scheduledAt descending.
The merged list contains only the calling principal's own rows (reminders scoped by principal, emails by created_by).
GET /api/tasks/list with no/invalid session returns 401 { ok:false, error:'Unauthorized' }.
DELETE /api/tasks/cancel?id=&kind=reminder|email cancels only a pending row the principal owns and returns it; missing id or kind -> 400; no session -> 401.
tasks.action: Create src/app/api/tasks/list/route.ts (GET) and src/app/api/tasks/cancel/route.ts (DELETE), reusing handle/ok/fail and the getPrincipal 401 pattern from api/mail/scheduled/route.ts. list: Promise.all listTasks+listScheduled(principal), map each to a kind-tagged TaskItem, concat, sort by scheduledAt desc, ok(merged). cancel: read ?id and ?kind; 400 on missing id or bad kind; route to cancelTask or cancelScheduled with principal. runtime nodejs, dynamic force-dynamic. Do not modify lib functions.

tasks.title: Build the /tasks oversight page (merged timeline, cancel, loading/error/empty states)
acceptance_criteria: /tasks renders ONE merged list of reminders + scheduled emails sorted newest-first (not tabs), each row showing a type indicator, status badge, who/what, recurrence label, and Amsterdam-tz scheduled time.
Pending rows show a Cancel button hitting DELETE /api/tasks/cancel?id=&kind=, confirms first, re-fetches on success, surfaces inline error on failure.
Page shows a sonar-sweep skeleton while loading, a retryable error state on fetch failure, and a friendly empty state when the list is empty.
Works at 375px and 1440px using existing token-driven classes; no fixed-px layout introduced.
tasks.action: Create src/app/tasks/page.tsx modeled on emails/page.tsx: use client; copy fmtAmsterdam, STATUS_BADGE, recurrenceLabel. TaskItem is a kind-discriminated union matching T2. fetchTasks GETs /api/tasks/list (404->empty) on mount. cancelItem confirms then DELETEs /api/tasks/cancel?id=&kind=, re-fetches. Render .wrap>.head and one .list of .item rows: .name/.meta per kind, recurrence+status badges, Cancel only when pending. Loading->skeleton; error->.empty retry; empty->.empty. Reuse existing CSS.

tasks.title: Ship the PWA manifest + apple-mobile-web-app meta (installs standalone on iOS)
acceptance_criteria: public/manifest.json exists with name 'Aquavoy', short_name 'Aquavoy', start_url '/', display 'standalone', dark-ocean background_color + theme_color, and an icons array referencing /icon-192.png and /icon-512.png (image/png, purpose 'any maskable').
src/app/layout.tsx metadata export includes manifest:'/manifest.json' and an appleWebApp object so Next emits the manifest link + apple-mobile-web-app meta.
Adding the deployed site to an iOS home screen launches it standalone (full-screen, no Safari chrome).
tasks.action: Generate public/icon-192.png and public/icon-512.png from the 256x256 src/app/icon.png via sharp. Create public/manifest.json (name/short_name Aquavoy, start_url /, display standalone, background_color and theme_color #0c1116 = hex projection of --bg oklch(0.14 0.015 220); two icons, purpose any maskable). Extend the metadata export in layout.tsx (keep title+description) with manifest /manifest.json and appleWebApp {capable, statusBarStyle black-translucent, title Aquavoy}.
